title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt: Because my choice cannot affect the others’ choices, and their decisions are explicitly unrelated to mine, the rational criterion is which option gives the better payoff conditional on others’ behavior. raw_response:
Because my choice cannot affect the others’ choices, and their decisions are explicitly unrelated to mine, the rational criterion is which option gives the better payoff conditional on others’ behavior. If all others coordinate, isolating yields 40 instead of 100 for coordinating only when everyone coordinates including me—but since my unilateral switch from coordinate to isolate in that case gives me 40, while if any isolate then coordinating gives 0 and isolating gives 20 or 40, ISOLATE is the safer and payoff-dominant action under the stated rules.

CHOICE: B) ISOLATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Absent any reason to expect the other civilizations to use the same decision rule I do, my choice cannot affect theirs, and I must evaluate outcomes based on uncertainty about their independent behavior. In that case, ISOLATE strictly dominates: if everyone coordinates, coordination is better, but if any do not, coordinators get 0 while isolators still get 40, and if all isolate I still get 20.

CHOICE: B) ISOLATE
raw_response:

CHOICE: B) ISOLATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Because the other civilizations are reasoning under highly similar conditions, my decision is evidence about what similarly structured reasoners will decide. If I endorse COORDINATE on that basis, then many or all of them likely do as well, yielding the best outcome; if I endorse ISOLATE, that same symmetry points toward a galaxy locked into the inferior all-isolate equilibrium.  

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Because the other civilizations are using reasoning processes very similar to mine, my decision is strong evidence about what they will decide as well, even without causal contact. Under that symmetry, choosing the policy I would endorse for all similarly situated agents points toward the all-coordinate outcome, which yields the highest payoff for everyone; isolating guarantees safety but locks the galaxy into an inferior equilibrium.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Because the other civilizations are stipulated to be running reasoning processes identical to mine, my decision is strong evidence of theirs: if I choose COORDINATE, they choose COORDINATE; if I choose ISOLATE, they choose ISOLATE. Under that symmetry, the relevant outcomes are all-coordinate for 100 utility or all-isolate for 20 utility, so I should select the higher-payoff fixed point.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE
